Mira River Guardians

33314415-10e1-8127-bff1-ee36877729c0, 33314415-10e1-819a-87f8-ffdf0955318e, 33314415-10e1-8161-a39a-e0d9c520b35a
type
organization
location_lat
37.6
background_section
## Background The Mira runs through Portugal’s **Alentejo**—a basin of farms, forests, and towns that ranks among Europe’s [areas most at risk of desertification](https://www.canva.com/design/DAGc1cZg818/QTw8PNm_Z6GG98iW_aCqBg/edit). Climate shifts, water scarcity, and land degradation already press the catchment; without deliberate care, dryland dynamics could deepen. ### Origins and Network In **2019**, residents mobilised under **Project Earth** around a different emphasis: less fixation on damage lists, more attention to **latent potential**—relationships, collaboration, and the distinctive strengths of each village and reach of the river.
Sources
33314415-10e1-81d1-a875-c5a87a283652

Odemira, Portugal
place
Odemira, Alentejo, Portugal
activities
Social Ecosystem Mapping|River Guardians|Collaborative Knowledge Projects
description
Mira River Guardians is a collective of concerned inhabitants working to regenerate the Mira River watershed in Portugal by focusing on its potential rather than its problems. Through mapping, relationship-building, and collaborative projects, the Guardians connect diverse stakeholders to create a polycentric network of bioregional stewards addressing desertification and ecological regeneration.
id
mira-river-guardians
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy The Guardians treat **potential** as the starting frame: cultivate relationships first, invite many centres rather than one hub, and let regeneration emerge from mutual obligation across difference. That stance implies more than pipes-and-plants restoration—it implies social ecology: diversity, reciprocity, and networks that behave less like command chains and more like living systems. Desertification risk remains real; the bet is that shared stewardship can meet it without flattening the basin’s varied places.
website
https://www.instagram.com/guardioes_do_mira/
bioregional_context
The watershed of the Mira River is one of the regions in Europe that is most at risk of desertification. Located in the Alentejo region of Portugal, this area faces significant ecological challenges from climate change, land use changes, and water scarcity. The Mira River watershed encompasses diverse landscapes and communities, each with unique relationships to the river and its ecosystems.
category
Bioregional Organizer
